summary: The patient’s unimproved condition necessitates effective treatment of potential generalized-onset or postpartum-related seizures; thus, IV carbamazepine is appropriate because it can be safely delivered through IV line and supports established use in such conditions while avoiding risks associated with other agents like oral valproate. This choice ensures both efficacy and safety based on pharmacological profile and clinical context.
